assertions.description: AudioContext is exposed on the global object.
BaseAudioContext.prototype exposes renderQuantumSize, the readable counterpart to renderSizeHint.
A default AudioContext reports renderQuantumSize as 128 frames.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;const c=new Ctx();const ok=c.renderQuantumSize===128;try{await c.close();}catch(_){}return ok;})()

assertions.description: AudioContextOptions.renderSizeHint accepts a numeric quantum hint.

assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;let c;try{c=new Ctx({renderSizeHint:'default'});}catch(_){return false;}const ok=c.renderQuantumSize===128;try{await c.close();}catch(_){}return ok;})()

assertions.description: renderSizeHint accepts the hardware keyword and reports a positive integer quantum.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;let c;try{c=new Ctx({renderSizeHint:'hardware'});}catch(_){return false;}const q=c.renderQuantumSize;try{await c.close();}catch(_){}return Number.isInteger(q)&&q>0;})()

assertions.description: OfflineAudioContext also accepts renderSizeHint in its options dictionary.
